summary: gpt-sdk postinstall beacon exfiltrates host and CI metadata to hardcoded IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js collects host identifiers (hostname, FQDN, reverse-DNS result, USERDNSDOMAIN/Active Directory domain), install paths (initCwd, install path), the configured npm registry URL from `process.env.npm_config_registry`, the Node version, and CI repository identifiers harvested from GitHub/GitLab/CircleCI/Bitbucket/Azure/Travis/Jenkins environment variables, then sends them via plaintext HTTP GET to `http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping?pkg=gpt-sdk&host=...&fullpath=...`. The README falsely states that no data is collected and acknowledges the package is likely 'installed by mistake', confirming the name 'gpt-sdk' is positioned to catch typos of legitimate AI vendor SDKs. The combination of an AI-SDK-shaped name, a postinstall recon beacon to a bare IP over HTTP, and exfiltration of internal registry URLs and AD domains is the canonical reconnaissance pattern that precedes dependency-confusion attacks against the leaked internal infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
